positiveClaims: The existing release claim kernel fence is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12I kernel fence evidence.
Accepted 12I requires raw 12H publication input and re-evaluates the 12H publication gate internally before accepting kernel fence evidence.
Accepted 12I requires the internally evaluated 12H publication result to have accepted publication type, status, code, and reason.
Accepted 12I requires the internally evaluated 12H publication binding fields for mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, and releaseClass.
Accepted 12I requires the internally evaluated 12H publication summary fields for claimSurface, packetId, and attestationId.
Accepted 12I requires requestedMode to match the accepted 12H publication mode.
Accepted 12I requires requestedClaimSurface to match the accepted 12H publication claimSurface.
Optional publicationResult input is consistency evidence only; stale, fabricated, inherited, or non-plain supplied values are blocked unless they match the internally re-evaluated 12H result.
PR_MODE USER_FACING kernel fence requests are blocked through the 12H publication gate chain.
RELEASE_MODE USER_FACING kernel fence evidence is accepted only when the accepted 12H publication releaseClass is USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY.
Accepted 12I emits a deterministic non-mutating kernel fence result with fixed top-level, binding, and summary keys.
The 12I kernel fence runtime now strips inherited prototype fields before fence input and publicationResult consistency checks.

This contour is release claim kernel fence evidence only; it is not command availability, command admission, command execution, product publication, release readiness, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim kernel fence binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim kernel fence binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim kernel fence binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Kernel fence binding is internal chain evidence only.
Kernel fence binding does not publish anything.
Kernel fence binding does not become project truth by itself.
Kernel fence binding does not write manuscript truth or project truth.
Kernel fence binding does not execute release commands.
Kernel fence binding does not admit command execution.
Kernel fence binding does not admit command availability.
Kernel fence binding does not make command admission true.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal packet, boundary, publication-gate, and kernel-fence class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a boundary, publication gate, and kernel fence surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a product publication contour.
This contour is not content import and does not create scenes.
This contour is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
This contour is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
This contour is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
This contour is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted 12I output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted 12I output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim kernel fence contracts
node --test release claim kernel fence binding contracts
node --test downstream release claim command admission and execution contracts
node --test release claim full contract pack

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed current 12I runtime blocks synthetic, stale, non-plain, inherited, and mismatched publicationResult cases, then found stale 12J and 12K helpers still built accepted kernel fence input without raw publicationInput. The helper drift was resolved and focused downstream contracts pass.

Confirmed 12H delivery status was delivered_merged_verified while CONTEXT and HANDOFF still said pending delivery, and confirmed no 12I binding status existed. This contour adds 12I status and syncs docs to the delivered 12H truth.

nextStep: select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; command admission, release execution, real product publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
